body:
## Why this matters

`projectbluefin/bluespeed` already implies three homelab host roles — NAS, media server, and general-purpose server — but this repo does not yet define what those roles must prove in the lab. We need a first issue that turns those role ideas into testable storage and persistence expectations.

## Scope

Define the first role/storage validation lane under #52:

- establish the initial role matrix for NAS, media-server, and general-purpose-server validation
- define the storage and state paths each role must preserve across reboot/update cycles
- call out where the current lab can validate the contract directly and where it needs blocker issues for shared-storage or RWX assumptions
- include the first storage observability hooks that materially affect role validation

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The first role matrix is defined for NAS, media server, and general-purpose server assumptions
- [ ] Each role lists the minimum storage/persistence behavior that must be validated
- [ ] Shared-storage or RWX gaps are surfaced explicitly instead of left implicit
- [ ] The lane is framed as validation of bluespeed role ideas, not as a replacement source of truth for them

## Links

- Child of #52
- Source ideas: `projectbluefin/bluespeed#7`, `projectbluefin/bluespeed#9`
title: feat: define homelab role matrix and storage-persistence contract
